data.title: King Risk Partners, 51st largest US insurance brokerage, adds Ten Eyck Group in NY for Northeast expansion.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, in Gainesville, Florida, King Risk Partners, the 51st largest insurance brokerage firm in the United States, revealed a major advancement in its Northeast expansion strategy. This was achieved by incorporating Ten Eyck Group as an affiliate. Ten Eyck Group, a prosperous entity in the insurance sector, will pave the way for increased market reach and opportunities in the region.

data.title: New Era unveils MLB 2026 caps, including Spring Collections for Batting Practice, Spring Training, and Clubhouse.
data.description: Unveiling the latest 2026 Spring Collections collaboration between New Era® and MLB, including Batting Practice, Spring Training, and Clubhouse LINKED HERE. Recently launched in Buffalo, N.Y. on Feb. 17, 2026, this collection represents the partnership between New Era and Major League Baseball (MLB), with New Era serving as the Official Cap of MLB.

data.title: Learn how to navigate an EU MDR Justification Document with Claigan webinar on Feb. 26th, 2026. Detailed examples provided.
data.description: In Ottawa, Ontario on February 26, 2026, Claigan is set to conduct a comprehensive webinar delving into the intricacies of an EU MDR Justification Document. This online event aims to provide a concrete demonstration of crafting a Justification Document for CMRs aligning with EU Medical standards. The webinar promises in-depth examples and practical insights into navigating the complexities of compliance with EU regulations. This session is crucial for professionals seeking clarity on the regulatory landscape, with attendees expected to surpass the thousands mark.

data.title: Map of 1300+ CA factory farms reveals hidden supply chains behind "humane" labels, thanks to FactoryFarmWatch.org.
data.description: FactoryFarmWatch.org has unveiled a groundbreaking map that serves as the ultimate repository of data concerning over 1300 industrial animal farms in California. Released by Direct Action Everywhere (DxE), a Berkeley-headquartered animal rights organization, this map stands as the first of its kind in providing a comprehensive overview of the state's factory farming landscape. The information, aggregated for the first time, will prove invaluable in shedding light on the operations of these facilities.

data.title: Fireplace raises $1.5M to create trading infrastructure for prediction markets, supported by Frachtis in pre-seed round.
data.description: A company based in Hong Kong called Fireplace, which specializes in trading terminals for prediction markets, recently revealed that they secured a pre-seed funding of $1.5 million. This investment will be used to enhance institutional trading infrastructure for a rapidly expanding asset class. The leading investor in this round was Frachtis.

data.title: ACDR welcomes Robert Sweeney as Government Affairs Manager for the East, with 20+ years of experience in financial services.
data.description: The Association for Consumer Debt Relief (ACDR) has welcomed Robert Sweeney as the Government Affairs Manager for the East region. With over 20 years of experience in state government affairs within the financial services sector, Sweeney's appointment aims to bolster advocacy efforts. Previously, he held the position of Government Affairs Officer for State Policy at the Investment Company Institute (ICI). ACDR, a prominent advocate for the debt relief industry, continues to lead nationwide initiatives.

data.title: Director Rinoj Varghese's independent filmmaking journey showcases confidence in bypassing gatekeepers, highlighting the importance of autonomy in the industry.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, in New York City, it was reported that Rinoj Varghese demonstrated a unique self-assurance by not seeking permission in the film industry. In an environment where gatekeepers hold significant power and aspirations are frequently put on hold outside studio premises, Varghese independently paved his way. He did not conform to traditional norms and rules, embodying a spirit of independence and determination.

data.title: Allegis and PDQ partner to launch SafeAxis™ panic hardware for industrial enclosures, ANSI Grade 1, Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: Allegis has recently introduced a new egress solution in collaboration with PDQ, offering ANSI Grade 1 certified and life-safety tested access. This innovative system is supported by Allegis' engineered approach to access solutions. The launch took place in Minneapolis on February 17, 2026. Serving industrial OEMs and enclosure manufacturers, Allegis continues to deliver high-quality products aimed at ensuring safety and security.

data.title: INB Homes launches Villas at King's Landing sales in Fort Pierce after construction kickoff event on Feb. 11.
data.description: Today, INB Homes revealed the availability of Villas at King's Landing for purchase in Fort Pierce, Florida. This announcement comes after a recent event on February 11th, where the commencement of construction was celebrated with the presence of local Realtors, business figures, and city officials. The development is now open for sale, offering a new housing opportunity in the area.

data.title: Ormat Technologies signs long-term geothermal Power Purchase Agreement with NV Energy to power Google sites in Nevada, enabling development of up to 150 MW capacity by 2030. Offer aligned with NV Energy’s Clean Transition Tariff. Approval anticipated in late 2026.
data.description: Ormat Technologies' stock (NYSE: ORA) experienced an increase after announcing a long-term geothermal Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with NV Energy to support Google's operations in Nevada. The agreement involves developing multiple geothermal projects in Nevada, enabling up to 150 megawatts of new geothermal capacity expected to be online between 2028 and 2030. The contract runs for 15 years after the final project achieves commercial operation, aligning with NV Energy's Clean Transition Tariff. Approval by the Public Utilities Commission of Nevada is anticipated in the second half of 2026.

data.title: Medtronic beats expectations in Q3 2026 with sales at $9.02 billion, 8.7% YoY increase and adjusted earnings of $1.36 per share.
data.description: Medtronic Plc (NYSE: MDT) released its third-quarter 2026 results, showcasing strong performance. The company reported sales of $9.02 billion, exceeding the anticipated $8.91 billion, marking an 8.7% year-over-year rise with 6% organic growth. Adjusted earnings stood at $1.36 per share, surpassing expectations of $1.33. Within its segments, the Cardiovascular Portfolio revenue reached $3.457 billion, up 13.8% reported and 10.6% organic, driven by increases in Cardiac Rhythm & Heart Failure, Structural Heart & Aortic, and Coronary & Peripheral Vascular. The Neuroscience Portfolio revenue hit $2.558 billion, up 4.1% reported and 2.5% organic, buoyed by growth in Neuromodulation and Cranial & Spinal Technologies. Specialty Therapies remained steady. For more details, refer to the full story on the latest Medtronic results.

data.title: Sun Auto Tire & Service expands with 2 acquisitions; now has 17 stores in Alabama. Strengthened presence in Gulf Coast and Alabama; MESA, Feb 17, 2026.
data.description: Sun Auto Tire & Service has recently bolstered its presence on the Gulf Coast by acquiring Bay City Tire & Wheel, which adds to their already-established 14-store network in the region. This acquisition provides a significant boost to their existing operations. Additionally, Black's Tire & Auto Save, operating across three locations, has extended its services to cover more areas in northeast and central Alabama. This development further expands their reach in the state. The expansion follows the company's recent growth strategy and solidifies their position in the market. The news was released in MESA, Ariz., on Feb. 17, 2026, indicating a milestone for Sun Auto Tire & Service.

data.title: MediKarma's study shows 50% A1C reduction using "Agentic AI" as alternative to ChatGPT; 20,000-unit deployment secured with FHC.
data.description: In a significant development with Dallas Endocrinology, it has been demonstrated that self-governing AI agents are capable of filling the void in care between medical visits. This innovation has resulted in remarkable outcomes, leading to a substantial deployment of 20,000 units with Family Health Choice (FHC). This achievement was confirmed on February 17, 2026, in San Francisco. MediKarma, a company utilizing AI technology, has successfully showcased the potential of autonomous AI agents in revolutionizing healthcare practices.
